metadata.notes: Country-level static dataset for first demo web application.

application_fee.notes: Standard German national visa fee (long‑stay visa) used as student visa fee【310840071292896†L242-L244】.

legal_work_hours_per_week.notes: International students may work up to 20 hours per week (140 full days/280 half-days per year) during term time【414181163000554†L310-L316】.

application_fee.notes: Approximate temporary residence permit fee (national residence permit for job‑seeking or graduate visa) €100–€110【918734663144154†L348-L355】.

duration_years.notes: Non‑EU graduates can obtain a residence permit to seek employment in Germany for up to 18 months【23428582805978†L320-L328】.

application_fee.notes: Approximate national residence permit fee for skilled workers (temporary) €100–€110【918734663144154†L348-L355】.

estimated_processing_years.notes: General estimate: after roughly five years of legal residence and employment a person may apply for permanent residence; processing times vary.

annual_tuition_fee.notes: Most public universities charge no tuition, only administrative fees of roughly €100–€350 per semester; we assume ~€1,500 per year to cover administrative fees and occasional tuition in Baden‑Württemberg or private programs【845758862525167†L368-L387】.

graduate_annual_salary.notes: Entry‑level IT specialist salary approximately €45,778 per year【63891457247544†L160-L180】.

mid_level_annual_salary.notes: Average IT specialist salary ~€63,647 per year【63891457247544†L160-L180】.

senior_annual_salary.notes: Senior IT specialist salary ~€71,661 per year【63891457247544†L160-L180】.

annual_salary_growth_rate.notes: Real wage growth in Germany was 1.8% year‑over‑year in March 2026 and expected to be around 1.5% by end‑2026【123606854292612†L133-L145】; we assume 2% annual salary growth for the model.

hourly_wage.notes: Germany’s minimum wage as of June 2026 is €13.90 per hour【765982900491821†L73-L94】; student part‑time jobs typically pay around this rate.

annual_salary.notes: Median gross income in Germany is €54,066 per year【765982900491821†L73-L94】; used as spouse salary assumption.

effective_income_tax_rate.notes: Average taxes and social contributions are roughly 35% of salary【765982900491821†L73-L94】.

employer_superannuation_rate.notes: Employer pension insurance contribution rate is 9.3% of gross salary【67437544897705†L460-L463】.

single_monthly.notes: Destatis consumption survey shows single‑person households spend €1,918 per month in total, including €817 on housing; subtracting housing yields €1,101 per month for all other expenses【828066178915918†L126-L138】.

couple_monthly.notes: Two‑person households spend €3,382 per month, including €1,240 on housing; non‑housing expenditure is €2,142 per month【828066178915918†L126-L138】.

family_with_one_child_monthly.notes: Three‑person households (two adults and one child) spend €4,081 per month total, including €1,434 on housing; non‑housing expenditure is €2,647 per month【828066178915918†L126-L138】.

single_monthly.notes: Average housing costs (rent, energy and maintenance) for single‑person households are €817 per month【828066178915918†L126-L138】.

family_monthly.notes: Average housing costs for three‑person households (family with one child) are €1,434 per month【828066178915918†L126-L138】.

monthly_cost_per_child.notes: Germany’s gross childcare costs are €552 per year【370955719470918†L806-L807】; dividing by 12 yields ~€46 per month.

public_transport_monthly.notes: The Deutschlandticket price increased to €63 per month in January 2026【503370179110941†L158-L160】.

monthly_cost.notes: Public health insurance for students under 30 costs about €148 per month in 2026【443402531435665†L585-L593】.

used_car_purchase_price.notes: Average price of a 3‑year‑old used car with 60,000 km was €20,032.1 in December 2025【485918469023133†L523-L525】.

annual_insurance.notes: Feather insurance lists partial comprehensive car insurance at €580–€636 per year【226540822993010†L124-L134】; we use €600 as an approximate annual premium.

annual_fuel.notes: Gasoline price around €2.17 per litre in May 2026【81037463141262†L133-L135】; assuming 8 L/100 km consumption and 15,000 km per year (1,200 L total), annual fuel cost ≈ €2,600.

annual_maintenance.notes: Maintenance and other running costs are not explicitly quantified; based on All About Berlin’s guidance on annual car costs (inspection, minor repairs, tax, parking) we assume €600 per year【664781528004315†L43-L79】.

annual_depreciation_rate.notes: Assume 15% depreciation per year for a used car.

car_loan_interest_rate.notes: Effective car loan rates in Germany range from 1.99% to 14.60%【884329008642536†L75-L81】; we use 4% as a typical rate.

inflation_rate.notes: Germany’s inflation rate was 2.6% in May 2026 and is projected to trend around 2.4% in 2027【95211453398490†L137-L166】.

savings_interest_rate.notes: ECB deposit rate as of 11 June 2026 is 2.25%【522249913866589†L91-L100】; used as a proxy for typical savings interest rate.

investment_return_rate.notes: Assume 5% annual investment return as a long‑term balanced portfolio assumption.

eur_to_lkr_exchange_rate.notes: Mid‑market rate on 28 June 2026 shows 1 EUR ≈ 383 LKR【957637124574866†L39-L45】.

education_loan_interest_rate.notes: KfW Studienkredit for students has a variable interest rate around 6.36% per annum【681248175434204†L269-L274】; used as education loan rate.

migration_loan_interest_rate.notes: Personal loan example shows a 4% interest rate【885521252361883†L930-L935】; used as migration loan rate assumption.

loans_and_debt.notes: If cash becomes negative, convert it into explicit debt instead of silently allowing negative cash.

sources.notes: Store URLs or citation labels here.
